name: Peewee
what_it_does: Lightweight Python ORM for SQLite, PostgreSQL, and MySQL — minimal, expressive ORM with Active Record pattern. Peewee features: Model subclasses as tables, Field types (CharField, IntegerField, ForeignKeyField, JSONField), ModelSelect query builder with .where()/.order_by()/.join(), database proxies for lazy initialization, migrations via peewee-migrate or playhouse.migrate, playhouse extensions (SQLiteExtDatabase, PostgresqlExtDatabase), atomic() context manager for transactions, chunked_models for large result sets, and in-process SQLite support. Ideal for scripts, CLIs, and lightweight agent services that don't need Django or SQLAlchemy's complexity.
use_cases: Agent state persistence in scripts — class Agent(Model): name = CharField(); status = CharField(default='idle'); Agent.create(name='search-agent') creates agent; Agent.select().where(Agent.status == 'idle') queries idle agents; peewee handles SQLite in single-file agent scripts

Lightweight agent microservice — Flask + Peewee + SQLite for agent tool tracking; no SQLAlchemy Session management; Agent.get_by_id(1) fetches agent; agent.save() persists changes; simple enough for agent tools team to maintain without ORM expertise

Agent configuration store — class AgentConfig(Model): agent_id = CharField(index=True); key = CharField(); value = JSONField(); Meta: database = SqliteDatabase('config.db') — agent config stored in local SQLite file without external DB dependency

Batch agent data processing — for row in Agent.select().where(...).order_by(Agent.created).iterator(): process(row) — .iterator() fetches rows lazily without loading all into memory; peewee chunked for processing thousands of agent records

Peewee with PostgreSQL for agent APIs — db = PostgresqlDatabase('agents', host='localhost', user='agent_user', password=os.environ['DB_PASS']); Agent.select().join(Task).where(Task.status == 'failed') — same API as SQLite for agent production PostgreSQL
not_for: Async agent backends — Peewee is synchronous; for async FastAPI agent services use SQLAlchemy async or Tortoise ORM
Complex ORM relationships — Peewee's query builder is simpler than SQLAlchemy; for complex agent domain models with polymorphism, mixins, and inheritance use SQLAlchemy
Enterprise Django apps — Django has its own ORM; Peewee doesn't integrate with Django admin, migrations, or management commands
best_when: Your Python agent script, CLI tool, or lightweight Flask/Bottle service needs database access without SQLAlchemy's complexity — Peewee is small enough to understand completely and expressive enough for most agent data persistence needs.
avoid_when: You need async support, complex relational modeling, Django integration, or auto-generated migrations — SQLAlchemy or Django ORM serve those needs better.

auth.notes: PostgreSQL/MySQL auth via connection parameters. SQLite needs no auth. Store credentials in environment variables.

pricing.notes: Peewee is MIT licensed, maintained by Charles Leifer. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits. SQLite single-writer limitation applies. Peewee generates efficient SQL; use .dicts() for faster dict results without model instantiation. .iterator() for memory-efficient large result sets.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: peewee.DoesNotExist raised by Model.get() when record not found. peewee.IntegrityError for unique constraint violations. OperationalError for DB connection issues. peewee.DatabaseError wraps driver-specific errors. No SQLAlchemy-style query explanation — use peewee.logger to log generated SQL.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Model.create() is not idempotent — creates new row each call. Model.get_or_create() is idempotent for lookup-or-create patterns. Model.insert().on_conflict_replace() provides upsert. bulk_create with conflict handling for batch idempotent inserts.
known_agent_gotchas: Database must be initialized before Model definition — db = SqliteDatabase('agent.db'); class Agent(Model): class Meta: database = db; db.connect() must be called before any query; agent scripts calling Agent.select() before db.connect() raise OperationalError; use database proxy for lazy init

Model.get() raises DoesNotExist not None — Agent.get(Agent.id == 999) raises Agent.DoesNotExist if not found; agent code expecting None return gets exception; use Agent.get_or_none(Agent.id == 999) for optional fetch or wrap in try/except

SQLite concurrency limited to single writer — multiple agent processes writing to same SQLite file cause 'database is locked' errors; use WAL mode (SqliteDatabase('agent.db', pragmas={'journal_mode': 'wal'})) to allow concurrent reads with single writer; for multi-process agent writes use PostgreSQL

ForeignKeyField doesn't fetch related by default — agent.task returns Task instance via lazy load (separate SQL query per access); agent code iterating 100 agents accessing agent.task makes 101 queries (N+1); use Agent.select().join(Task) or prefetch(agents, Task) to avoid agent N+1 queries

Peewee doesn't have built-in migrations — peewee-migrate or playhouse.migrate.migrate() provide schema migration; no built-in migration tracking like Django or Alembic; agent schema changes require manual migration scripts; use peewee-migrate for version-tracked agent schema evolution

Closed connection not auto-reconnected — Peewee connections don't auto-reconnect after PostgreSQL timeout; long-running agent scripts hit 'InterfaceError: connection already closed'; use db.connection_context() or call db.connect(reuse_if_open=True) at start of each agent task

security_score_components.security_notes: Peewee parameterizes queries by default — .where(Agent.name == name) generates SQL with %s placeholder, not string interpolation. Raw SQL via Model.raw('SELECT...') is vulnerable to injection — avoid raw queries with user input. Store database passwords in environment variables.
